exercises.title: Sort a CSV file
exercises.description: Use the system sort command to sort a CSV by one column and inspect the result.
exercises.expected_outcome: Rows should be ordered by the chosen column.

exercises.title: Compare random and nearly sorted input
exercises.description: Measure how long your chosen sort takes on random input versus almost-sorted input.
exercises.expected_outcome: Nearly sorted input should often be faster for adaptive sorts like timsort.

exercises.title: Implement quicksort
exercises.description: Write an in-place quicksort and verify it works across random and adversarial inputs.
exercises.expected_outcome: The array should end up sorted, with uneven partitions handled correctly.

exercises.title: Implement stable mergesort
exercises.description: Write a stable mergesort and confirm equal keys keep their original order.
exercises.expected_outcome: Equal elements should retain their relative order after sorting.

exercises.title: Build an external merge sort
exercises.description: Sort a data set larger than memory by chunking, sorting chunks, and merging them on disk.
exercises.expected_outcome: The final output should be sorted without loading the full input into memory.

exercises.title: Benchmark bounded-integer sorts
exercises.description: Compare counting sort with a comparison sort on integer keys from a small range.
exercises.expected_outcome: Counting sort should win when the key range is small relative to n.
